subject: [RFC PATCH v1 1/3] iomap: add ->iomap_next() and iomap_process() helper

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested adding function typedefs for consistency and questioned the need to pass separate iomap and srcmap arguments, instead suggesting passing only the iter argument.
reviews.raw_body:
If you add function typedefs for all the other two methods I'd one one here
as well for consistency.  We'll need it anywone once the legacy ops are
entirely gone we can kill of the ops and just pass this function pointer.

As for the arguments:  I don't really see much of a point in passing
the iomap/scrmap separately vs just the iter.  Or am I missing something
here?

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a potential issue with callback modifying internal fields, agreed to simplify iomap_next() by dropping two arguments and making it non-const.
reviews.raw_body:
It was meant to safeguard against a callback modifying iomap-internal
non-mapping fields (iter->pos/len/status/iter_start_pos) but maybe
this is unnecessary. I'll drop the 2 args and make it non-const.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-06-25.patch_summary: This patch adds a new callback function called ->iomap_next() to the iomap iteration mechanism, which replaces the existing ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() callbacks. The new callback allows performance-critical callers to inline the iteration with a fixed callback, improving efficiency. A helper function called iomap_process() is also added to simplify the logic needed in an ->iomap_next() implementation. This change enables filesystems to be converted one at a time from using the legacy ->iomap_begin()/->iomap_end() path to the new ->iomap_next() callback.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the iomap_iter() function should maintain its current version without changes to constness, but expressed concern about potential future issues with non-const data in the iter private structure.
reviews.raw_body:
As in my reply to Gao I missed the constness point.  So I think keeping
the current version should be ok.  I'm a little worried that we might
end up with something not beeing const in the end like the iter private
data, though.

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch adds a new callback function called ->iomap_next() to the iomap iteration mechanism, which replaces the existing ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() callbacks. The new callback allows performance-critical callers to inline the iteration with a fixed callback, improving efficiency. A helper function called iomap_process() is also added to simplify the logic needed in an ->iomap_next() implementation. This change enables filesystems to be converted one at a time from using the legacy ->iomap_begin()/->iomap_end() path to the new ->iomap_next() callback.
